what_it_does: Implements a file-discovered registry for mode-detection rules: it loads rule modules from configured directories, validates their declared data-AST conditions against the shared rules grammar, and exposes rules in explicit priority order for a detector to evaluate against activity-signal snapshots.
observations: Rules are loaded from .py files in directories via importlib.util, with underscore-prefixed files skipped.
Each rule module must declare a top-level MODE_DETECTION_RULE dict; missing declarations are silently skipped.
Rule validation is fail-loud: bad id, missing fields, unknown fields, wrong types, or an invalid when AST all raise immediately.
The rule id must equal the module filename, making each rule addressable by file.
The when condition is a data-AST in the rules.RULE_OPS grammar; it is validated at discovery and evaluated later.
Candidate mode validity is deferred to detect time, not checked at discovery, because the registry cannot see the live mode set.
Ordering is by declared integer priority (lower first), then id as a deterministic tiebreak — not by filename or discovery order.
The registry provides dict-like access, rediscovery for removal, and serialization of the original spec verbatim.
standouts: Explicit priority field decouples first-match-wins semantics from filesystem ordering.
Reuses the same rules.RULE_OPS predicate engine used elsewhere instead of introducing lambdas or eval.
Bool is explicitly rejected as a priority value despite being an int subclass.
Extensive inline documentation frames the file as a constitution/drift-home for the registry.
conventions: Follows the standalone registry pattern seen in projections.py, roles.py, and registry.py.
Uses dataclasses for the rule representation and private helper functions for loading and validation.
Fail-loud validation with descriptive error messages.
rediscover clears and rebuilds so file removal unregisters rules.
concerns: discover executes arbitrary .py files from the directory, which is intentional but relies on filesystem trust.
register silently overwrites an existing rule with the same id without warning.
Candidate mode is not validated at discovery, so an invalid candidate is only caught at detect time.
No duplicate-id handling across multiple discovery directories.
notes: This is the registry side of the mode auto-detector: it discovers and validates rule files, but leaves actual mode-switching authority to the existing toggle.
questions: What is the exact shape of the activity_signal snapshot passed to matches?
Where are the mode_detection_rules directory paths configured and passed to discover?
How does activation.detect_mode_candidate consume ordered() and enforce candidate membership in MODES?
What is the full grammar and operator set of rules.RULE_OPS?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a standalone, file-discovered, validated, priority-ordered rule registry with no hardcoded rules or side effects.
apparent_themes: self-contained mirror of existing registry patterns
intention_signals: The file is trying to make mode-detection heuristics authorable as droppable data files, evaluated by the same predicate engine used elsewhere, while keeping the detector itself effect-free.
novelty: The explicit integer priority field that decouples first-match-wins ordering from filename or discovery order, and the reuse of the G3 rules data-AST for runtime signal predicates.
contribution: A validated, ordered, file-discovered registry of mode-detection rules and a pure evaluation method that the detector can call without re-implementing rule loading or predicate parsing.
summary_for_embedding: A file-discovered registry and dataclass for mode-detection rules that validate, order by priority, and evaluate data-AST conditions against activity-signal snapshots.
intention_for_embedding: To let mode-detection heuristics be added as data files rather than code edits, using the shared rules engine and keeping the detector effect-free.

symbols.does: Dataclass representing one discovered detection rule.
symbols.description: Holds id, candidate, why, when AST, priority, and the original spec; provides pure evaluation against a signal and serialization as a record.

symbols.does: Evaluates the rule's when AST against an activity signal.
symbols.description: Calls rules.evaluate with _validated=True, returning a boolean; intended to be pure and deterministic.

symbols.does: Returns the rule's original declared dict.
symbols.description: Serializes the discovered spec verbatim for surfaces or projections.

symbols.does: Imports a rule file by path.
symbols.description: Uses importlib.util to load a .py module from the registry directory and returns its filename stem and module object.

symbols.does: Validates and constructs a ModeDetectionRule from a module declaration.
symbols.description: Checks id, required fields, types, unknown fields, and validates the when AST with rules.validate_ast, raising on any malformed entry.
Mirrors other registries; loads modules from directories, registers valid rules, supports rediscovery, and exposes rules in priority order plus dict-like access.
Creates an empty dict to hold discovered rules keyed by id.

symbols.does: Loads rule files from the given directories.
symbols.description: Iterates sorted .py files (excluding underscore-prefixed), imports each, and registers any module declaring MODE_DETECTION_RULE.

symbols.does: Rebuilds the registry from the filesystem.
symbols.description: Clears the rule map and re-runs discover so removed rule files unregister.

symbols.does: Adds a validated rule declaration to the registry.
symbols.description: Delegates to _build_rule and stores the resulting ModeDetectionRule under the file stem.
Sorts by ascending priority then id; this is the order the detector walks.

symbols.does: Returns all rules as plain dicts in firing order.
symbols.description: Maps ordered rules through as_record for serialization.
Dict-like access to the internal rules map.

symbols.does: Tests whether a rule id is registered.

symbols.description: Yields keys from the internal rules map.

symbols.does: Returns the number of registered rules.
symbols.description: Reports the size of the internal rules map.

symbols.does: Looks up a rule by id with a default.
